text: I stand before a glass door, peering into a modern space bathed in soft light and colored by abstract patterns and screens. The world outside this room feels calm, clinical, almost expectant. Yet, anticipation tinges the air. As I step inside, surrounded by the R+D exhibition, I feel my curiosity intensify. This is a place of human thought, invention, and reflection—a sanctuary for the meeting of minds and machines. Here, amid panels about human-robot interaction, I am both observer and participant. I feel awed by the potential that is unfolding and slightly unsettled by the uncertain contours of our technological future. There is wonder and a trace of unease—how readily shall we humans adapt to sharing existence with our own creations?
